# Sensitivity Analysis — raw vs cleaned data

*Generated by `python -m emerald_ai sensitivity`, seed 20260609. Tests whether the headline
findings survive the rule-based data cleaning (54 impossible values → NaN).*

## Bake-off PR-AUC (median across 25 folds)
| index | combo | PR-AUC raw | PR-AUC cleaned | Δ |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | dummy+none | 0.0128 | 0.0128 | 0.0 |
| 1 | logreg+class_weight | 0.1165 | 0.1171 | 0.0006 |
| 2 | logreg+smote | 0.1177 | 0.1174 | -0.0002 |
| 3 | xgboost+class_weight | 0.0912 | 0.0927 | 0.0015 |
| 4 | xgboost+smote | 0.0909 | 0.1048 | 0.014 |

## Single-feature PR-AUC — does cleaning rescue Credit Score?
| index | feature | PR-AUC raw | PR-AUC cleaned |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | Credit Score | 0.0104 | 0.0104 |
| 1 | Revenue | 0.0645 | 0.0645 |
| 2 | Time In Business | 0.0113 | 0.0114 |

## Verdict
- **RQ1 robustness:** cleaning does NOT overturn the no-winner
  conclusion — logistic regression still ≥ XGBoost on PR-AUC.
- **Credit Score finding:** raw 0.0104 → cleaned 0.0104
  (prevalence floor ≈ 0.0128).
  Credit Score remains weak even after cleaning — the feature itself carries little signal here, not just the 0-coding.

The findings are reported on **cleaned data by default**; this table documents that the cleaning
does not manufacture or destroy the result.

---
*Reproduce: `python -m emerald_ai sensitivity`*
